tasks.description: Extend Ranking schema in packages/domain/src/Benchmark.ts: add optional fields rlm: Schema.optional(Schema.Boolean), rlmDepth: Schema.optional(Schema.Number), rlmAttempts: Schema.optional(Schema.Number). These are informational metadata fields — the model ID string (with /rlm suffix) is the primary discriminator. No other schema changes needed.
tasks.acceptance: tsc --noEmit passes across all packages; Ranking type includes the three optional fields

tasks.description: Create apps/server/src/llm/OpenRouterClient.ts. Effect Context.Tag service with Layer. Interface: { complete: (modelId: string, messages: ReadonlyArray<ChatMessage>) => Effect<string, LlmError> }. ChatMessage = { role: 'user'|'assistant'|'system'; content: string }. LlmError tagged class. Layer reads OPENROUTER_API_KEY from process.env. Uses HttpClient (BunHttpClient) + HttpClientRequest.post to https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions with body { model, messages, max_tokens: 2048 }. Response decoded via Schema.decodeUnknownEffect. Maps HTTP 4xx/5xx and missing choices to LlmError. Export: OpenRouterClient tag, OpenRouterClientLive layer, LlmError class, ChatMessage type.
tasks.acceptance: Type-checks; Layer can be provided in tests via mock HttpClient

tasks.description: Create apps/server/src/llm/LlmPrompts.ts. Pure TypeScript functions (no Effect — fully unit-testable). Three exports: (1) buildSolvePrompt(task: Task): string — system message asking model to solve the lambda calculus task, includes task.desc and all task.tests formatted as expr / = want pairs, instructs to reply with ONLY @main = <lambda-expr>. (2) buildRetryPrompt(task: Task, priorAttempt: string, errors: ReadonlyArray<string>): string — adds prior attempt and lam checker error lines to context, asks for a corrected solution. (3) buildTaskDetectionProbe(taskPreview: string, n: number): string — the exact λ-RLM Phase 2 7-option digit menu prompt from the reference, adapted: metadata includes length=n and preview of task description.
tasks.acceptance: All three functions are pure; no imports from Effect; test-friendly

tasks.description: Create apps/server/src/rlm/LambdaPlan.ts. Faithful TypeScript port of reference lambda_rlm.py _plan() method. Exports: TaskType enum (SUMMARIZATION|QA|TRANSLATION|CLASSIFICATION|EXTRACTION|ANALYSIS|GENERAL), ComposeOp enum (6 values), PipelineFlags type { useFilter: boolean }, LambdaPlan type { taskType, composeOp, pipeline, kStar, tauStar, depth, costEstimate, n }, COMPOSITION_TABLE, PLAN_TABLE, C_COMPOSE, C_IN constants (identical values to reference). plan(taskType, n, contextWindowChars?, accuracyTarget?, aLeaf?, aCompose?): LambdaPlan pure function implementing the exact reference formula: k*=ceil(sqrt(n*C_IN/c_compose)), d=ceil(log_k*(n/K)), accuracy constraint loop, tau*=min(K,floor(n/k*)), cost estimate. Defaults: contextWindowChars=100_000, accuracyTarget=0.80, aLeaf=0.95, aCompose=0.90.
tasks.acceptance: plan(GENERAL, 500, 100_000) returns depth=0 kStar=1; plan(GENERAL, 200_000, 100_000) returns depth>=1 kStar>=2; pure function, no Effect imports

tasks.description: Create apps/server/src/rlm/LamCodeExtractor.ts. Pure function extractLamCode(raw: string): string. Priority order: (1) content inside ```lambda...``` or ```lam...``` fence — strip fence markers and trim; (2) find first line matching /^@main\s*=/ in raw text, take that line and all subsequent lines until blank line or end; (3) raw string trimmed as fallback. No Effect imports.
tasks.acceptance: Extracts correctly from fenced code, bare @main lines, and falls back to raw

tasks.description: Create apps/server/src/rlm/LambdaRlm.ts. Main λ-RLM class as an Effect service. Config type LambdaRlmConfig: { modelId: string; contextWindowChars: number; accuracyTarget: number; aLeaf: number; aCompose: number; maxDepth: number }. Reads LLM_MODEL (default 'minimax/minimax-m2.5:free') and RLM_MAX_DEPTH (default 3) from process.env. Implements the 5-phase algorithm from the reference as Effect.gen functions: Phase 1: context_0 stored as a string (no REPL socket needed — direct in-memory). Phase 2: one LLM call via OpenRouterClient using buildTaskDetectionProbe, parse digit → TaskType. Phase 3: plan(taskType, n, contextWindowChars, ...) → LambdaPlan — 0 LLM calls. Phase 4: log cost estimate via Effect.log. Phase 5: executeΦ recursive function — for lambda tasks (depth=0 from plan), effectiveDepth = max(plan.depth, config.maxDepth) drives self-correction iterations. Φ(context: string, depthRemaining: number): on leaf (depthRemaining==0 or len<=tauStar): call LLM via buildSolvePrompt or buildRetryPrompt → extractLamCode → runTask (lam checker as oracle) → return LlmCheckResult. On non-leaf: split(context, kStar) → Effect.all(chunks.map(Φ(..., d-1)), {concurrency: kStar}) → reduce (for lambda: selectBest by pass then by fewest errors). Main export: rlmEval = Effect.fn('rlmEval')(function*(task, refBits?)): Effect<LlmCheckResult, never, OpenRouterClient | FileSystem | Path>. LlmCheckResult extends CheckResult with { attempts: number; depth: number }.
tasks.acceptance: Type-checks; rlmEval with maxDepth=1 makes exactly 2 LLM calls (1 probe + 1 leaf); rlmEval with maxDepth=0 makes exactly 1 LLM call (probe) then 1 leaf; stop early on pass

tasks.description: Extend apps/server/src/check/Check.ts with two new exports. (1) TimedCheckResult type = CheckResult & { elapsedMs: number }. (2) runTaskWithLlm = Effect.fn('runTaskWithLlm')(function*(task: Task, modelId: string, refBits?: number)): Effect<TimedCheckResult, never, OpenRouterClient | FileSystem | Path> — records Date.now() start, calls OpenRouterClient.complete with buildSolvePrompt(task) as a single user message, extracts lam code, calls runTask, returns result with timing. (3) runAllTasksForModel = Effect.fn('runAllTasksForModel')(function*(tasks, modelId, refBitsMap, concurrency?)): Effect<ReadonlyArray<TimedCheckResult>, never, ...> — uses Effect.forEach with { concurrency: concurrency ?? 4 }. All existing exports remain unchanged.
tasks.acceptance: Existing exports unchanged; new functions type-check; concurrency default is 4
Written file is parseable by existing parseResultFile in BuildResults.ts; new variant/rlm_depth/rlm_attempts lines added without breaking existing regex

tasks.description: Extend apps/server/src/build/BuildResults.ts. (1) Add variant: 'standard' | 'rlm' and rlmDepth?: number and rlmAttempts?: number to RunResult type. (2) Extend parseResultFile to read: variant line (default 'standard'), rlm_depth line (parse as number), rlm_attempts line (parse as number). (3) Extend the rankings builder in build() to set rlm: true, rlmDepth, rlmAttempts on Ranking when run.variant === 'rlm'. (4) In latestPerModel, the model string already discriminates standard vs RLM (different model IDs) so no change needed to deduplication logic. All existing behavior preserved.
tasks.acceptance: build() still produces valid BenchmarkData for existing res/ files; new fields appear on RLM-variant rankings

phases.name: Eval orchestrator and CLI run command

tasks.description: Create apps/server/src/eval/ModelEvalRunner.ts. Export runModelEval = Effect.fn('runModelEval')(function*(model: TopModel, tasks, refBitsMap)): Effect<void, never, OpenRouterClient | FileSystem | Path>. Runs standard and RLM evals concurrently via Effect.all([standardRun, rlmRun], { concurrency: 2 }). Standard run: runAllTasksForModel(tasks, model.modelId, refBitsMap) → writeResultFile(model.modelId, results, 'standard'). RLM run: for each task call rlmEval(task, refBitsMap.get(task.id)) using model.modelId (LambdaRlm reads LLM_MODEL from env but we pass modelId explicitly as config) → collect → writeResultFile(model.modelId + '/rlm', results, 'rlm', { depth, attempts }). Export also: loadRefBitsMap = Effect.fn that loads all reference bits from lam/ dir into a ReadonlyMap<string, number>.
tasks.acceptance: Standard and RLM writes happen concurrently; RLM model ID = base + '/rlm' in result file

tasks.description: Extend apps/server/src/index.ts with a 'run' command. Add 'run' to parseArgs union type. Add runCommand = Effect.gen that: reads TOP_MODELS_FILE env → loads top-models.json → loads all tasks (loadAllTasks) → loads refBitsMap (loadRefBitsMap) → for each model in sequence calls runModelEval(model, tasks, refBitsMap). Provide OpenRouterClient layer (OpenRouterClientLive) alongside existing BunServices and BunHttpClient in BunRuntime.runMain. Update CLI header comment to document the run command and new env vars LLM_MODEL and RLM_MAX_DEPTH.
tasks.acceptance: bun src/index.ts run starts without type errors; layers compose correctly; existing eval and build commands unchanged

tasks.description: Create apps/client/src/lib/fmt.ts. Export fmtModel(m: string): string — strips 'openrouter/' prefix if present, then if remaining string ends with '/rlm' replace that with ' [rlm]'. Example: 'openrouter/minimax/minimax-m2.5:free/rlm' → 'minimax/minimax-m2.5:free [rlm]'. 'openrouter/google/gemini-2.5-pro' → 'google/gemini-2.5-pro'. 'google/gemini-2.5-pro' (no prefix) → 'google/gemini-2.5-pro'. Pure function, no imports.
tasks.acceptance: Pure function; handles prefix-absent and prefix-present cases; [rlm] appended correctly

tasks.description: Update all 5 leaderboard panels + MatrixPanel to import fmtModel from '@/lib/fmt' instead of defining it locally. Files: IntelligencePanel.tsx, SpeedPanel.tsx, ElegancePanel.tsx, ValuePanel.tsx, MatrixPanel.tsx. Remove the local fmtModel function definition from each file and add the import. No other changes to these files — zero data-flow changes, zero layout changes. The [rlm] suffix is naturally accommodated since all panels already use fmtModel for display and allocate width dynamically (maxNameLen computed from actual names).
tasks.acceptance: No local fmtModel in any of the 5 panel files; all import from @/lib/fmt; tsc passes

tasks.description: Create apps/server/src/llm/OpenRouterClient.test.ts using vitest (matching server vitest.config.ts pattern). Mock HttpClient layer using same pattern as reference eval-runner.test.ts (HttpClient.make + Layer.succeed). Tests: (1) happy path — complete() returns choices[0].message.content from mock JSON response; (2) HTTP 4xx response body → LlmError with message; (3) response with empty choices array → LlmError; (4) correct URL used (openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions); (5) Authorization header includes Bearer token.
tasks.acceptance: 5 tests pass via bun test --filter=server

tasks.description: Create apps/server/src/llm/LlmPrompts.test.ts using vitest. Pure unit tests (no Effect, no mocks needed). Tests: (1) buildSolvePrompt includes task.desc; (2) buildSolvePrompt includes each test expr and want; (3) buildSolvePrompt instructs reply with @main =; (4) buildRetryPrompt contains priorAttempt string; (5) buildRetryPrompt contains all error strings; (6) buildTaskDetectionProbe contains all 7 menu items (1-7 digit options); (7) buildTaskDetectionProbe includes n and preview in metadata.
tasks.acceptance: 7 tests pass

tasks.description: Create apps/server/src/rlm/LambdaPlan.test.ts using vitest. Pure unit tests. Tests: (1) plan(GENERAL, 500, 100_000) returns depth=0, kStar=1, tauStar=500 (fits in window); (2) plan(GENERAL, 200_000, 100_000) returns depth>=1, kStar>=2 (requires splitting); (3) k_star formula: plan with c_compose=2.0 (MERGE_SUMMARIES) vs 0.01 (CONCATENATE) produces different kStar; (4) accuracy constraint: with low aLeaf=0.5, aCompose=0.5, kStar increases to satisfy target; (5) cost estimate > 0 for any valid input; (6) depth=0 fast-path: when n<=K returns pipeline and compose_op from table.
tasks.acceptance: 6 tests pass

tasks.description: Create apps/server/src/rlm/LamCodeExtractor.test.ts using vitest. Pure unit tests. Tests: (1) extracts from ```lambda fence; (2) extracts from ```lam fence; (3) extracts bare @main = line from prose paragraphs; (4) returns trimmed raw when no fence and no @main line; (5) multiline lambda expression after @main = is included; (6) ignores ```python fences (not lambda/lam).
tasks.acceptance: 6 tests pass

tasks.description: Create apps/server/src/rlm/LambdaRlm.test.ts using @effect/vitest (it.effect pattern). Mock layers for OpenRouterClient and the lam runner. Tests: (1) maxDepth=0 — makes exactly 1 task-detection LLM call + 1 leaf call = 2 total, returns pass:false on mock failure; (2) maxDepth=1 first attempt passes — stops immediately, attempts=1 on the leaf; (3) maxDepth=2 first fails second passes — attempts=2 on leaf (total LLM calls = 1 probe + 2 leaf = 3); (4) all maxDepth=2 attempts fail — pass:false, attempts=2; (5) plan() called with correct n (length of task prompt); (6) Phase 2 detection fires before Phase 5 Φ (verify call order via mock spy).
tasks.acceptance: 6 tests pass via bun test --filter=server

tasks.description: Create apps/client/src/lib/fmt.test.ts using vitest (client vitest.config.ts, browser mode). Tests: (1) strips openrouter/ prefix; (2) appends [rlm] for /rlm suffix; (3) both prefix strip and [rlm] append together; (4) no openrouter/ prefix — returned as-is; (5) no /rlm suffix — no [rlm] appended; (6) google/gemini-2.5-pro stays google/gemini-2.5-pro.
tasks.acceptance: 6 tests pass via bun test --filter=client

tasks.description: Extend apps/client/src/lib/atoms/benchmark-atom.test.ts. Add to the existing describe('computeValueEntries') block: (1) model with /rlm suffix is included in value entries with correct model string preserved; (2) RLM variant alongside base model — both appear as separate entries; (3) sort order is stable when RLM and base model have identical passPerDollar.
tasks.acceptance: 3 new tests + existing 6 tests all pass

tasks.description: Update .env.example at repo root: add OPENROUTER_API_KEY=your_key_here, LLM_MODEL=minimax/minimax-m2.5:free, RLM_MAX_DEPTH=3 under a new # LamBench RLM section. Also update apps/server/AGENTS.md run command table with the new 'run' command and the three new env vars. No functional code changes.
tasks.acceptance: .env.example contains all three new vars with defaults
